title: A Fiber-Aware Extension to the Minimum Viable, Auditable (MVA) Framework for Counter-UAS Assessment
subtitle: Link-Typed, Distance-Segmented, and Phase-Aware Evaluation under Mixed Fiber–EM Threat Conditions
title_normalized: a fiber-aware extension to the minimum viable, auditable (mva) framework for counter-uas assessment

description: This working paper extends the Minimum Viable, Auditable (MVA) framework for counter-UAS assessment by introducing a fiber-aware, link-segmented threat regime. It addresses the structural limitation of EM-centric counter-UAS evaluation when fiber-guided or hybrid-link unmanned aerial systems bypass electromagnetic countermeasures within bounded operational envelopes. The extension preserves MVA's core outcome metrics while clarifying where EM measures remain relevant as phase-specific filtering layers.
abstract: The Minimum Viable, Auditable (MVA) framework was developed to evaluate counter–unmanned aerial system (C-UAS) effectiveness under conditions of cost asymmetry, saturation, and mission-level endurance, with a primary analytical focus on electromagnetic (EM) countermeasures. The operational deployment of fiber-guided unmanned aerial systems (UAS), however, exposes a structural limitation in this assumption. Fiber guidance does not merely reduce the effectiveness of EM countermeasures; it effectively bypasses their applicability within bounded operational envelopes. This paper introduces a minimal, auditable extension to the MVA framework that accounts for fiber-guided and hybrid-link UAS without invalidating the original model. The extension formalizes a link-typed, distance-segmented, and phase-aware threat regime, replacing fixed range assumptions with an operational fiber feasibility envelope. Three distance–link scenarios—fiber-dominant, hybrid transition, and extended or relay-based—are defined, each associated with distinct defensive leverage points and cost dynamics. By repositioning EM measures from universal termination tools to stage-specific filtering layers, the proposed patch preserves MVA’s core outcome metrics, such as cost per loss avoided, key asset preservation, mission continuity, and cost endurance, while restoring analytical validity under mixed fiber–EM threat conditions. The resulting framework remains minimal, auditable, and extensible as control-link technologies evolve.

methodology.framework_name: Fiber-Aware Extension to the Minimum Viable, Auditable (MVA) Framework
methodology.base_framework: Minimum Viable, Auditable (MVA) Framework for Counter-UAS Assessment
methodology.core_problem: EM-centric C-UAS evaluation assumes electromagnetic addressability, but fiber-guided UAS can bypass EM countermeasures within bounded operational envelopes.
new_structural_components: Repositioning of EM countermeasures as stage-specific filtering layers

main_variables.name: Mission distance
main_variables.description: Distance between the launch or control origin and the defended asset.

main_variables.name: Operational fiber feasibility envelope
main_variables.description: Scenario-dependent maximum distance over which fiber-based control remains tactically viable for a given platform and mission configuration.

main_variables.name: Transition multiplier
main_variables.description: Scenario-dependent multiplier used for classifying hybrid transition regimes; not a fixed technological constant.

distance_link_regimes.name: Fiber-Dominant Zone
distance_link_regimes.defensive_leverage: Close-range kinetic engagement, corridor control, passive protection, decoys, and architectural resilience; EM measures have little direct leverage over the primary control loop.

distance_link_regimes.name: Hybrid Transition Zone
distance_link_regimes.defensive_leverage: Phase-synchronized EM effects regain localized relevance during control-link or navigation-mode transitions.

distance_link_regimes.name: Extended / Relay-Based Zone
distance_link_regimes.defensive_leverage: Defensive leverage shifts toward relay nodes, coordination links, synchronization mechanisms, and system-of-systems disruption.

dimensions.name: Control-Link Type
dimensions.description: Distinguishes fiber-guided, electromagnetic, hybrid, and relay-based control configurations.

dimensions.name: Threat Distance
dimensions.description: Mission distance used to classify whether fiber guidance remains dominant, transitions to hybrid operation, or requires relay/extended packages.

dimensions.name: Operational Fiber Feasibility Envelope
dimensions.description: Bounded operational envelope of tactically viable fiber control, conditioned by platform, terrain, maneuver demand, and environment.

dimensions.name: Phase-Aware Defensive Attribution
dimensions.description: Attribution of defensive effects according to where along the engagement sequence they reduce burden, filter threats, preserve assets, or avoid losses.

dimensions.name: MVA Outcome Metrics
dimensions.description: Preserved outcome metrics including cost per loss avoided, key asset preservation, mission continuity, and cost endurance.

key_findings: Fiber guidance does not merely degrade EM countermeasure effectiveness; it can remove EM applicability within bounded operational envelopes.
The MVA framework remains valid if control-link heterogeneity is modeled upstream of outcome metric calculation.
A fixed range assumption should be replaced by an operational fiber feasibility envelope conditioned by platform, terrain, maneuver, and environmental factors.
Fiber-dominant, hybrid transition, and extended / relay-based zones create different defensive leverage points and cost dynamics.
EM countermeasures should be evaluated as phase-specific filtering layers rather than universal termination tools under mixed fiber–EM threat conditions.
Preserving MVA metrics while adding link-typed and phase-aware threat classification restores analytical validity without wholesale framework replacement.
Failure to account for fiber-guided control regimes risks overstating defensive effectiveness, misallocating resources, and biasing procurement and doctrine development.

figures.title: MVA evaluation flow with the fiber-aware, link-segmented threat regime inserted upstream of outcome metric calculation
figures.description: Flowchart showing that core MVA metrics are preserved while a structural extension inserts the operational fiber feasibility envelope and distance–link segmented regime before mixed-threat scenario classification and metric calculation. The figure differentiates fiber-dominant, hybrid transition, and extended / relay zones and links them to phase-aware attribution and MVA outcome metrics.
figures.source: Author’s analysis based on the MVA framework
